name: Mountain River
description_short: Power Credit Union (formerly Mountain River) is a member-owned, not-for-profit credit union serving Southern Colorado with checking, savings, lending, and digital banking services across multiple branches.
description_long:
Power Credit Union is a federally insured, member-owned credit union operating in Southern Colorado. The organization recently completed a system conversion and merger, welcoming members from Mountain River Credit Union into the Power Credit Union platform. The credit union operates multiple branch locations including newly expanded service areas in Alamosa, Buena Vista, and Salida, Colorado, reflecting recent growth in their service territory.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of deposit and lending products. On the deposit side, they provide multiple checking account tiers (Power Checking, Empowered Checking, Jump Start Teen, Premier 50+), personal savings accounts, money market accounts, Christmas Club accounts, and share certificates. For lending, they offer Visa credit cards with rates starting at 8.90% APR, auto loans, home mortgage loans, home equity lines of credit (6.49% APR), RV and boat loans, personal loans, and specialized products like Holiday Loans and Bankruptcy Auto Rebuilder Loans. They also provide convenience services including a 5,000+ shared branch network, 30,000+ shared ATM network, online and mobile banking, ACH early posting, and wire transfers.

Key distinguishing features include their recent expansion into three new Colorado communities, active community involvement programs, and their Green Path Financial Wellness educational resources. The website highlights positive member testimonials emphasizing personalized service and staff responsiveness. They offer specialized tools like loan calculators, financial wellness coaching, and fraud awareness education. The recent merger integration positions them as a growing regional credit union with improved technology infrastructure and expanded service capacity.

As a credit union, membership is required to access services. While they offer competitive rates on both credit cards and home equity products, actual rates vary based on creditworthiness and membership qualification. The recent system conversion may create short-term technical considerations for members transitioning from Mountain River accounts, though they provide dedicated resource pages for this process.
services: Personal checking accounts (Power, Empowered, Jump Start Teen, Premier 50+)
Personal savings accounts, money market accounts, Christmas Club, and share certificates
Visa credit cards with rates from 8.90% APR, no annual fees
Auto loans with pre-approved local financing
Home mortgage loans and home equity loans/lines of credit
Online and mobile banking with 24/7 access
5,000+ shared branch network and 30,000+ shared ATM network
Wire transfers, ACH early posting, check reorder services
Green Path Financial Wellness education and fraud awareness programs
Bankruptcy auto rebuilder loans and loan payment center services
pros: Access to 5,000+ shared branch locations and 30,000+ ATM network nationwide through CO-OP shared branching
Competitive credit card rates starting at 8.90% APR with no annual fees or balance transfer fees
Home equity loans and lines of credit with rates as low as 6.49% APR
Multiple specialized checking accounts including teen accounts (Jump Start) and senior accounts (Premier 50+)
Comprehensive lending products including auto, RV, boat, motorcycle, and manufactured home loans
24/7 online and mobile banking access with digital banking tools and financial calculators
Green Path Financial Wellness program providing financial education and fraud awareness resources
cons: Recent system conversion from Mountain River merger may cause temporary account access or transition issues despite provided resources
Membership eligibility requirements limit access compared to traditional banks—not all consumers can open accounts
Limited branch presence in Southern Colorado specifically (only 3 new locations listed) despite statewide shared branching access
No information provided about deposit insurance limits, emergency loan access, or lines of credit for existing members
Credit approval and rates vary based on creditworthiness; advertised rates may not be available to all applicants
best_for: Southern Colorado residents seeking personalized service with competitive rates on home equity and auto lending
Credit union members prioritizing access to 5,000+ branch locations and nationwide shared ATM networks
Consumers interested in credit builder and youth banking programs (Jump Start Teen accounts, Smart Start Youth Savings)
Homeowners looking for home equity financing with rates below 7% APR
diagnosis: Power Credit Union is best for Southern Colorado residents and anyone seeking credit union membership benefits, including access to extensive shared branching networks and competitive lending rates. The primary caveat is that credit union membership eligibility may restrict access, and the recent Mountain River merger integration may cause temporary transition considerations for existing members switching to the new platform.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
